# RHAISTRAT-1737: llm-d Deployment Template Selection in Model Serving Wizard

## Metadata

- **Type**: Feature
- **Status**: In Progress
- **Labels**: dashboard-zaffre-scrum, rfe-creator-auto-created, rfe-creator-autofix-rubric-pass, rfe-creator-feasibility-pass, rfe-creator-needs-attention, strat-creator-3.5, strat-creator-auto-created, strat-creator-auto-refined, strat-creator-human-sign-off, strat-creator-rubric-pass, test-plan-auto-created, test-plan-rubric-pass
- **Components**: AI Core Dashboard, Documentation, llm-d, Serving Orchestration, UXD

## Description

h2. Business Need (from RFE)

{quote}The full business need is maintained in the source RFE: [RHAIRFE-2208|https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHAIRFE-2208]{quote}

h2. Strategy (AI Generated by Agentic SDLC Pipeline)

h3. TL;DR

Add llm-d deployment template support to the RHOAI Dashboard through two major parts: (1) a topology selector in the model serving wizard presenting four deployment patterns backed by labeled and annotated LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources with an advanced routing section, and (2) admin settings pages for managing topology-type and router LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources via YAML editors with backend sample templates. Additionally, consolidate Legacy, LLM-D, and vLLM on MaaS LLM resources into the generative UI and present HardwareProfile as an accelerator selector for single-node topology only. This is a Tech Preview feature.

*Components*: odh-dashboard (host app, gen-ai BFF), odh-model-controller (model-serving-api), KServe (LLMInferenceServiceConfig manifests)

h3. Technical Approach

The RHOAI Dashboard currently falls back to a raw YAML editor for LLMInferenceService deployments. POC customers (Taikang, Verizon, PureCS) have reported they cannot deploy llm-d models from the GUI. The feature comprises two major parts: wizard inclusions for LLM-D template support and admin UIs for managing the underlying LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources. This is a Tech Preview feature -- scope is appropriately bounded to validated patterns with YAML-editor-based admin UIs rather than rich form rendering.

*1. LLMInferenceServiceConfig Type Convention*

The feature is built on a type system for LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources using a label and an annotation:

* {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} (label): Declares the config's role. Valid values are {{workload-single-node}}, {{workload-multi-node-data-parallel}}, {{workload-single-node-pd}}, {{workload-multi-node-data-parallel-pd}}, {{router}}, and {{accelerator}}.
* {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} (annotation): Used on router and accelerator configs to indicate which topology types the config supports, e.g., {{'["workload-single-node", "workload-multi-node-data-parallel"]'}}. This is an annotation (not a label) because the value is a JSON array, which exceeds Kubernetes label value format restrictions.

The dashboard lists LLMInferenceServiceConfigs via K8s API with a label selector on {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} for server-side filtering, then reads the {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation client-side for router/accelerator association. KServe ships well-known configs (e.g., {{config-llm-template}}) in {{config/llmisvcconfig/}} -- these configs must be updated with the appropriate labels and annotations. This is a KServe manifest change, not a code change.

*2. Wizard Topology Selector*

When a user deploys a generative model via LLM-D, the wizard presents a topology selector defaulting to single-node. Four topology types are available, each mapping to specific KServe preset configs via {{spec.baseRefs}}:

* *Single-node* ({{workload-single-node}}): Enabled by default using KServe's shipped configs. Produces a CR with {{spec.baseRefs}} referencing the {{config-llm-template}} preset. This is the only topology that exposes the HardwareProfile accelerator selector, since single-node deployments manage resources at the pod level. Non-single-node HWP is a known TP limitation.
* *Multi-node data-parallel* ({{workload-multi-node-data-parallel}}): Disabled unless at least one admin-created config of this type exists. Produces a CR with {{spec.baseRefs}} referencing the selected config plus {{config-llm-worker-data-parallel}}. Adds {{spec.worker}} to the generated CR for LeaderWorkerSet orchestration.
* *Single-node P/D disaggregate* ({{workload-single-node-pd}}): Disabled unless admin configs exist. Produces a CR with {{spec.baseRefs}} referencing {{config-llm-prefill-template}} and {{config-llm-decode-template}} presets. Adds {{spec.prefill}} for separate prefill/decode workloads.
* *Multi-node P/D disaggregate* ({{workload-multi-node-data-parallel-pd}}): Disabled unless admin configs exist. Combines prefill worker ({{config-llm-prefill-worker-data-parallel}}) and decode worker ({{config-llm-decode-worker-data-parallel}}) presets.

Disabled topology types are shown grayed-out with guidance that admin configuration is required, directing the user to the admin settings page.

An *advanced routing section* is available within the wizard. When expanded, it lists router LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources filtered by the selected topology type. The filtering reads the {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation on each router config and checks whether the selected topology type is in the JSON array. The user can optionally select a router config to include in the generated CR's {{baseRefs}}.

*CR generation* uses {{spec.baseRefs}} to reference selected configs. Configs are merged in baseRefs order (last wins on conflicts), and inline spec fields take highest precedence. The admin YAML editor must account for LLMInferenceServiceConfig webhook constraints: the KServe validator rejects configs that include the forbidden {{baseRefs}} field and rejects configs with invalid scheduler replicas (must be >0).

*3. Admin UIs for LLMInferenceServiceConfig Management*

Two new settings pages are added to the main navigation, adjacent to the existing ServingRuntimes page:

* *Topology Configs page*: Lists LLMInferenceServiceConfigs where {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} matches a topology-type value. The create button is a combo-create that lets the admin select which topology type to create. Create, edit, and duplicate flows use a YAML editor as the primary editing interface. This is appropriate for TP -- rich form rendering is deferred.
* *Router Configs page*: Lists LLMInferenceServiceConfigs where {{opendatahub.io/config-type: router}}. Create, edit, and duplicate flows with a YAML editor. The topology association is managed via the {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation.

Each config type supports fetching a starter sample from a backend API: {{GET /api/v1/samples/llm-d?type=<config-type>[&topology=<topology>]}}. This API is hosted on the model-serving-api server in odh-model-controller, which already exposes {{/api/v1/gateways}}. The sample provides a static pre-populated YAML template with sensible defaults -- templates are hardcoded in Go, not dynamically generated. This is appropriate for TP scope. The samples API uses the same Bearer token + SelfSubjectAccessReview auth pattern as the existing gateways endpoint.

Consolidating these admin UIs into a single tabbed interface alongside ServingRuntimes is out of scope and covered by RHAISTRAT-1281.

*4. Existing Wizard Adjustments*

* *Generative UI consolidation*: Legacy model serving, LLM-D, and vLLM on MaaS LLM resources are now all surfaced in the generative model serving UI. The legacy checkbox from the previous wizard page is relocated into the generative flow, creating a single entry point for all generative model deployments.
* *HardwareProfile as Accelerator Selector*: When the user selects single-node LLM-D topology, the HardwareProfile selection is presented as an accelerator selector. This addresses an ask-miss from RHOAI 3.4 when vLLM on MaaS was added without HWP support for LLM-D. Non-single-node topologies hide HWP and accelerator elements entirely, since resource allocation for those topologies is managed through the LLMInferenceServiceConfig. This is a known TP limitation.

*5. Dashboard BFF Integration*

The gen-ai BFF does not use informer watches for LLMInferenceServiceConfigs. Instead, the wizard and admin pages make K8s API list calls via the existing pass-through proxy ({{/api/k8s/*}} on the dashboard backend or {{/api/v1/k8s/*}} on the gen-ai BFF). List calls use label selectors for {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} to filter server-side. The {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation is read client-side from the list response. This is the standard dashboard data-fetching pattern -- no new BFF endpoints or controllers are needed for config discovery.

*6. Minimum Shippable Scope (Cut-Line)*

If the L estimate proves tight, work streams should be cut in this order:

* *P0 (must ship)*: Wizard topology selector with single-node default + all 4 types, admin topology configs page, admin router configs page, backend samples API, CR generation with baseRefs
* *P1 (cut first)*: HWP-as-accelerator-selector for single-node, generative UI consolidation, advanced routing section in wizard
* The P1 items are technically separable. However, the generative UI consolidation provides the wizard entry point for LLM-D -- if deferred, the existing YAML fallback remains functional.

*Backward Compatibility*: Existing InferenceService and ServingRuntime wizard flows remain unchanged. The topology selector and admin UIs are additive. Manual LLMInferenceService CR creation via kubectl/YAML is unaffected.

h3. Affected Components

||Component||Change||Owner Team||
|odh-dashboard (frontend)|Topology selector in wizard; admin list/create/edit/duplicate pages for LLMInferenceServiceConfigs; generative UI consolidation; HWP as accelerator selector|Dashboard Team|
|odh-dashboard (gen-ai BFF)|K8s API list calls for LLMInferenceServiceConfigs with label filtering via existing proxy; annotation parsing client-side|Dashboard Team|
|odh-model-controller (model-serving-api)|Add samples API endpoint ({{/api/v1/samples/llm-d}}) to model-serving-api server|Model Serving Team|
|KServe (LLMInferenceServiceConfig manifests)|Add {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} label and {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation to shipped configs in {{config/llmisvcconfig/}}|KServe Team|

h3. Impacted Teams

||Team||Components Owned||Involvement||
|Dashboard Team|odh-dashboard|Primary implementer: topology selector, admin UIs, wizard adjustments, BFF list calls|
|Model Serving Team|odh-model-controller|Implement samples API endpoint in model-serving-api server|
|KServe Team|KServe, llmisvc-controller|Add labels/annotations to shipped LLMInferenceServiceConfig manifests|
|UXD Team|N/A|Design: topology selector, admin page layouts, guidance copy|

h3. High Level Requirements

* [P0] User can select a deployment topology from four options when deploying a generative model via LLM-D
* [P0] Single-node topology is available by default using KServe's shipped configs
* [P0] Non-single-node topologies are disabled when no matching admin-created configs exist
* [P0] Generated LLMInferenceService CR includes {{spec.baseRefs}} referencing selected topology and router configs
* [P0] Admin can list, create, edit, and duplicate topology-type LLMInferenceServiceConfigs via settings page
* [P0] Admin can list, create, edit, and duplicate router LLMInferenceServiceConfigs via settings page
* [P0] User can successfully deploy a model end-to-end using a selected topology
* [P1] HardwareProfile/accelerator selector shown only for single-node topology
* [P1] Advanced routing section filters router configs by selected topology type via annotation
* [P1] Legacy, LLM-D, and vLLM on MaaS resources unified in generative UI
* [P1] Create/edit pages pre-populate YAML editor with sample from backend API
* [P1] No degradation to existing wizard capabilities (InferenceService/ServingRuntime flows)

h3. Dependencies

||Dependency||Type||Status||Impact if Blocked||
|KServe LLMInferenceService CRD (v1alpha2)|Internal|Exists|Wizard cannot create LLMInferenceService CRs|
|KServe LLMInferenceServiceConfig CRD|Internal|Exists|Admin pages cannot manage configs|
|{{opendatahub.io/config-type}} labels on shipped KServe configs|Internal|Needs implementation|Topology selector cannot determine available deployment patterns; KServe manifest change required in {{config/llmisvcconfig/}}|
|{{spec.baseRefs}} merge behavior in llmisvc-controller|Internal|Exists|Generated CRs would not inherit config templates correctly|
|HardwareProfile CRD (infrastructure.opendatahub.io)|Internal|Exists (GA since 3.3)|Accelerator selection for single-node would not work|
|odh-model-controller model-serving-api server|Internal|Exists|Samples API needs new route added to existing server|
|UXD design for topology selector and admin pages|Internal|Needed|Development blocked on visual design|

h3. Non-Functional Requirements

* *Performance*: Topology selector adds one K8s API list call (LLMInferenceServiceConfigs with label selector) to wizard initialization. Admin pages use standard paginated list patterns. CR creation is a single K8s API POST via existing pass-through. (architecture context: odh-dashboard data flows)
* *Security*: CR creation uses existing user token pass-through via K8s API proxy, enforcing the user's RBAC. Admin pages use the same pass-through pattern -- no new RBAC entries are needed since vLLM on MaaS already configured the required permissions for LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources. Samples API uses existing model-serving-api auth (Bearer token + SelfSubjectAccessReview). (Staff Engineer / SME Input; architecture context: odh-dashboard Authentication & Authorization; overlay 0011: gateway discovery server)
* *Backwards Compatibility*: Existing InferenceService and ServingRuntime wizard flows must remain unchanged. Users who create LLMInferenceService CRs via kubectl/YAML are unaffected. (RFE acceptance criteria)
* *Scalability*: LLMInferenceServiceConfig list calls scale with the number of admin-created configs per namespace. For TP, no load target is specified -- a typical namespace will have fewer than 20 configs. (No explicit load target in RFE or architecture context)

h3. Out-of-Scope

* *Tabbed serving admin UI*: Consolidating ServingRuntimes and LLMInferenceServiceConfig admin pages into a single tabbed interface -- covered by RHAISTRAT-1281
* *Rich form rendering for config editing*: Admin create/edit uses YAML editor; structured form rendering from config schemas is deferred (appropriate for TP)
* *Advanced scheduler tuning in wizard*: Scheduler plugin weights, flow control policies, and EPP configuration are managed through router configs, not exposed as individual wizard fields
* *Model Registry integration*: Selecting models from Model Registry for LLMInferenceService deployment is out of scope
* *Dynamic template discovery beyond type conventions*: Topology cards are driven by {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} labels; arbitrary custom discovery mechanisms are deferred
* *Dynamic samples based on cluster hardware*: Samples are static Go-embedded templates appropriate for TP; hardware-aware sample generation is deferred

h3. Acceptance Criteria (Proposed -- requires PM/Engineering validation)

Given a user navigating to the model serving wizard and selecting a generative model type, when the wizard reaches the deployment pattern step, then the user sees a topology selector with single-node enabled by default and non-single-node topologies disabled if no admin configs exist, measured by UI element state checks against LLMInferenceServiceConfig list results.

Given a user selecting the single-node topology and providing a hardware profile, when the user completes the wizard and submits, then an LLMInferenceService CR is created with {{spec.baseRefs}} including a reference to the shipped single-node config ({{config-llm-template}}) and the selected hardware profile's resource requirements, measured by verifying the CR via {{kubectl get llminferenceservice -o yaml}}.

Given an admin who has created a multi-node data-parallel LLMInferenceServiceConfig, when a user selects the multi-node data-parallel topology and completes the wizard, then an LLMInferenceService CR is created with {{spec.baseRefs}} referencing the admin config and {{config-llm-worker-data-parallel}}, and {{spec.worker}} is present in the CR, measured by CR structure validation.

Given an admin who has created P/D disaggregate LLMInferenceServiceConfigs, when a user selects the single-node P/D disaggregate topology and completes the wizard, then an LLMInferenceService CR is created with {{spec.baseRefs}} referencing {{config-llm-prefill-template}} and {{config-llm-decode-template}}, and {{spec.prefill}} is present in the CR, measured by CR structure validation.

Given a user selecting a non-single-node topology, when the topology is selected, then the HardwareProfile and accelerator selector fields are hidden, measured by UI element visibility checks.

Given an admin navigating to the topology configs settings page, when the admin clicks create and selects a topology type, then the YAML editor is pre-populated with a sample config from the backend samples API, measured by the create page rendering with non-empty YAML content.

Given an admin editing an existing LLMInferenceServiceConfig via the admin page, when the admin submits YAML containing a {{baseRefs}} field, then the submission fails with a validation error from the KServe webhook, measured by error message display in the YAML editor.

Given a user expanding the advanced routing section in the wizard after selecting single-node topology, when router configs exist with {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation containing {{workload-single-node}}, then those router configs are listed as selectable options, measured by list content matching annotation-filtered configs.

Given a user deploying a model via InferenceService or ServingRuntime, when the user completes the existing wizard flow, then the wizard behavior is identical to the pre-change baseline, measured by existing E2E test suite passing without modification.

Given the LLMInferenceService CRD is not installed on the cluster, when a user opens the model serving wizard, then the LLM-D topology selection is not shown, measured by CRD availability check gating the wizard step.

h3. Effort Estimate

*Size: L (Large, 6-9 sprints)*

Justification: The work includes: (1) wizard topology selector with four types, conditional field rendering, and advanced routing section (2 sprints); (2) two new admin settings pages with list/create/edit/duplicate flows backed by YAML editors and sample templates (3 sprints); (3) backend samples API in odh-model-controller model-serving-api (0.5 sprint); (4) KServe manifest changes to add labels/annotations to shipped configs (0.5 sprint); (5) existing wizard adjustments -- generative UI consolidation and HWP-as-accelerator-selector (1 sprint); (6) cross-cutting: UXD coordination, E2E testing across wizard flows, regression testing (1.5 sprints). Two primary teams involved (Dashboard, Model Serving), with KServe team contributing manifest changes. The admin UIs follow existing ServingRuntime page patterns, reducing design risk.

h3. Risks

||Risk||Impact||Mitigation||
|{{baseRefs}} merge semantics change in future KServe releases|Wizard-generated CRs inherit incorrect or no config, breaking deployments|Add integration test validating {{baseRefs}} resolution for each topology type; pin behavior to documented merge semantics|
|{{opendatahub.io/config-type}} label convention becomes a cross-team API contract that KServe must maintain|If KServe changes config naming or removes labels, wizard and admin pages break|Define label values in a shared contract document with CI validation; add test asserting shipped configs have correct labels|
|LLMInferenceService CRD (v1alpha2) schema changes before GA|Wizard produces CRs with stale or invalid fields|Abstract CR generation behind a version-aware builder; track v1alpha2 stability timeline|
|UXD design delays for admin pages and topology selector|Development blocked on visual design|Begin with PatternFly 6 card/table patterns using placeholder layouts; integrate final designs when available|
|Samples API not available at wizard development time|Admin create flows cannot pre-populate YAML editors|Develop create flows with empty YAML editor; add sample pre-population when API is ready|
|Admin submits YAML violating LLMInferenceServiceConfig webhook constraints|Confusing error experience for admin users|Surface KServe webhook validation errors clearly in the YAML editor UI; document known constraints (no baseRefs field, scheduler replicas > 0)|

h3. Assumptions

* KServe's shipped LLMInferenceServiceConfigs will adopt the {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} label and {{opendatahub.io/supported-topologies}} annotation conventions for 3.5 -- needs validation with KServe team
* The {{spec.baseRefs}} merge behavior (last config wins, inline spec takes precedence) will remain stable through 3.5 -- needs validation
* The odh-model-controller model-serving-api server can host the samples API endpoint without architectural changes -- confirmed (server already exposes {{/api/v1/gateways}})
* HardwareProfile CRDs are available and populated in the cluster -- confirmed (infrastructure.opendatahub.io HardwareProfile GA since 3.3, overlay 0005)
* No new dashboard RBAC entries are needed for LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources -- confirmed (Staff Engineer / SME Input: vLLM on MaaS already configured all needed k8s permissions)
* Samples are static Go-embedded templates -- confirmed (Staff Engineer / SME Input: samples should just be samples, TP feature)

h3. Open Questions

||Question||Why It Matters||Owner||
|What are the exact {{opendatahub.io/config-type}} label values on shipped KServe configs, and when will the manifests be updated?|Wizard topology selector depends on these values; this is a blocking KServe manifest change|KServe Team|
|What is the minimum hardware recommendation text for each topology type?|Topology selector guidance text needs accurate "use when" information|llm-d Team|
|Which KServe preset configs map to each topology type for {{baseRefs}} generation?|Wizard must produce correct {{baseRefs}} combination per topology or deployments fail|KServe Team + Dashboard Team|
|What validation error messaging should the admin YAML editor display for webhook rejections?|LLMInferenceServiceConfig webhook constraints (no baseRefs field, scheduler replicas > 0) need clear error UX|Dashboard Team + UXD Team|

h2. Staff Engineer Input
